message:
chore(beads): configure sync branch and update sync baseline

Configure beads synchronization infrastructure:

- Set sync-branch to "main" in .beads/config.yaml
  This enables `bd sync` to work without requiring the
  BEADS_SYNC_BRANCH environment variable or manual
  configuration each session. Previously the sync.branch
  setting was commented out with a default example of
  "beads-sync", but this project syncs directly to main.

- Update sync_base.jsonl with 17 new baseline entries
  The sync baseline file stores the last-known-good state
  for 3-way merge operations during `bd sync`. This update
  brings the baseline current with the latest issue tracking
  state (376 total issues tracked).

These changes support the beads-based issue tracking workflow
described in AGENTS.md, ensuring agents can run `bd sync` at
session end without additional configuration steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@
 # This setting persists across clones (unlike database config which is gitignored).
 # Can also use BEADS_SYNC_BRANCH env var for local override.
 # If not set, bd sync will require you to run 'bd config set sync.branch <branch>'.
-# sync-branch: "beads-sync"
+sync-branch: "main"
 
 # Multi-repo configuration (experimental - bd-307)
 # Allows hydrating from multiple repositories and routing writes to the correct JSONL
@@ -59,4 +59,4 @@
 # - linear.url
 # - linear.api-key
 # - github.org
-# - github.repo
+# - github.repo
\ No newline at end of file
